title: Lock card height for virtualization math + document constraint
description: Virtualizer requires uniform card height to compute the visible window without measuring each card. Current cards are uniform; pin this as an invariant in CLAUDE.md.
acceptanceCriteria: All track cards render with a fixed height (CSS: .card { height: 72px; box-sizing: border-box; } - or whatever the current uniform height is).
Vitest test asserts every card in a rendered set has the same offsetHeight.
CLAUDE.md (or .claude/project/web-ui.md) documents: 'Track cards must remain uniform height for virtualization. Variable heights require measured-height virtualization.'
Future card features that need expansion (e.g., inline expand) must use overlays or modals, NOT in-list expansion.

steps.details: Open docs/index.html, inspect .card CSS. If height is implicit (line-height-driven), pin it explicitly: .card { height: 72px; }. Adjust value to match current visual height.
Define CARD_HEIGHT_PX = 72 (or computed value) at top of script. Virtualizer.attach receives itemHeight: CARD_HEIGHT_PX.
tests/web/ui-logic.test.js: render 100 cards in jsdom; assert all have the same getBoundingClientRect().height (jsdom may not compute this; use offsetHeight after setting explicit CSS).
.claude/project/web-ui.md (or CLAUDE.md): 'Card height is locked at CARD_HEIGHT_PX (TASK-033). The virtualizer assumes uniformity. Inline expand is forbidden; use overlay or modal.'

technicalNotes: If a future feature truly needs variable height: switch to a measured virtualizer (e.g., react-window's variable-size patterns) but stay in vanilla JS.
